_doc_claude_code_env_vars: This file configures claude-hooks itself, NOT Claude Code.
Claude Code env vars go in ~/.claude/settings.json under 'env'.
For the full curated reference with per-var verdicts see
docs/env-vars.md. Quick summary of the most useful ones:
CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_BACKGROUND_TASKS=1
Recommended. Disables the subagent 'Warmup' drain that can
eat 99%+ of daily input tokens on plugin-heavy setups. See
issue #47922. Side-effect: also disables Ctrl+B and the Bash
tool's run_in_background parameter.
CLAUDE_CODE_AUTO_COMPACT_WINDOW=400000
Forces autocompact at 400k tokens. Recommended by bcherny
(Anthropic staff) if you feel Opus 4.6 degrades at large
contexts.
CLAUDE_AUTOCOMPACT_PCT_OVERRIDE=75
Autocompact at 75% fill. Aggressive but useful for long
sessions.
CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_ADAPTIVE_THINKING=1  +  MAX_THINKING_TOKENS=63999
bcherny-stack adaptive-thinking disable. We did NOT find it
helpful on this project (more trivial mistakes, slower
iteration). docs/env-vars.md has the long form.
ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_OPUS_MODEL=claude-opus-4-5-20251101
Pins Opus to 4.5 if you feel 4.6 regressed. We kept 4.6.
CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_1M_CONTEXT=1
Reverts Opus context to 200k. Big token saver on Max plans
but we rely on 1M for this codebase.
Example settings.json env block (copy into ~/.claude/settings.json,
NOT into this file):
"env": {
"CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_BACKGROUND_TASKS": "1"

_doc: Postgres + pgvector. See docs/pgvector-runbook.md for the full
compose+migrate walkthrough, the swap-the-embedder recipe, and
the benchmark numbers behind the default pick.
Default embedder is qwen3-embedding:0.6b (1024 dim, native 32k
ctx). Picked over nomic-embed-text after the 2026-04-28 bench:
tighter cosine distances on niche queries, better semantic recall
on architecture/intent prompts, and the 32k native ctx eliminates
the silent truncation that hits long Stop summaries on 8k-ctx
models. Speed cost is real (~85 ms p50 embed vs ~38 ms for nomic)
but absolute total recall stays under 100 ms end-to-end on HNSW.
Tables are model-namespaced — the embedding column is
vector(<dim>), so each model gets its own memories_<short> and
kg_observations_<short> tables. Run
python scripts/migrate_to_pgvector.py --embedder qwen3
to populate them on first install. ⚠ NEVER change the model in
this config without re-embedding the corpus first — recalls
against the wrong vector space return garbage with no error.
Other supported models: nomic (768 dim, 8k ctx, faster);
arctic (1024 dim, 8k ctx, slowest); minilm (384 dim, 256 ctx —
avoid, the truncation kills recall on real memories).
additional_tables hybrid-recalls across the kg_observations
(formerly Memory-KG) table at the same model. Suffixes must
match — table=memories_qwen3 ↔ additional_tables[0]=kg_observations_qwen3.

session_start.compact_recall_query: session context, key decisions, and important patterns

caliber_proxy._doc: Opt-in local OpenAI-compat proxy that routes caliber (https://github.com/caliber-ai-org/ai-setup) to a local Ollama model with project grounding + tool-use. When enabled, install.py writes a systemd unit (caliber-grounding-proxy.service) bound to 127.0.0.1:38090. Point caliber at it with OPENAI_BASE_URL=http://127.0.0.1:38090/v1. Env knobs (override in systemd drop-in): CALIBER_GROUNDING_UPSTREAM, CALIBER_GROUNDING_MODEL_OVERRIDE, CALIBER_GROUNDING_THINK, CALIBER_GROUNDING_FORCE_ANSWER_AFTER, CALIBER_GROUNDING_MAX_TOOL_CALLS_PER_TURN. See claude_hooks/caliber_proxy/ and docs/gemma4-tool-use-notes.md.
